calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Amended Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Sep-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER CRAIG SEGALL's Hearing On Amended Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed petition for declaratory relief is granted. Based on the evidence presented and for good cause shown, the court finds and declares as follows:

a) Petitioner's deceased great-great grandfather, Szlama-Zelman Lopatka, is one and the same person as Salomon Lepetkin, Schlama Lopatke, and Soloman Segall.
b) Petitioner's deceased great-great grandmother, Hana Lopatka is one and the same person as Hanna Monroe, Hannah Manda, and Hana Mondral.
c) Petitioner's great-grandfather, Charles Segall is one and the same person as Hil-Majer Lopatka, Berl Lopatke, Charles Meyer Segall, Charles Mayer Segall, Charles M Segall, and Chas M Segall.
d) Petitioner's grandfather, Solomon Segall is one and the same person as Soloman S Segall, Solomon Segall Segall and Solomon Sidney Segall.

Petitioner may submit a proposed judgment that conforms to this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER CRAIG SEGALL'S Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief.

Craig Holt Segall presents a verified petition asking the court to declare his line of ancestry. The court takes judicial notice of the documents he has presented but denies relief without prejudice. The court lacks genealogical expertise or a reasoned basis to conclude that the date of birth of July 18, 1892, a place of birth shown alternatively as Radzan or Radzanow, and parents whose names are, at best, arguably similar is sufficient basis to decide that Berl Lopatke is the same person as Hil-Majer Lopatka (whose birth date is also listed as July 30, 1892) and Charles Segall.

Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
